I read the prompt as fixing the special submission format: distinguished tame generators sigma,tau, implicit tame relation ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080. I treated the prior investigations as a proposal log. The only candidate not yet refuted in that log is still the dynamic tame-inertia word recorded in Turn 68.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant here because this exponent is applied only to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

What I regard as solid from the prior log Two formulas should be kept as equations, not informal fractions.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, if t is the deck involution of the sign double cover and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character, then forNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ the Fox coefficientDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít.ThusDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument work: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, 1‚ąít is radical, so Da is radical and ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑ is a unit. Hence nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0 up to the harmless T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1 convention. The legitimate elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.Then h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, since for ő∂f=1, ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1),and 2s+1 is coprime to f. With these conventions, the previously established candidate-side checks remain coherent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.New Turn 69 contribution: primitive residual descent without cyclotomic division

Turn 68 gave the exact-order test in the formő¶c (U)‚ą£ŌĀc (R)(U),where R(T)=‚ąĎj=0d‚ąí1rjTj‚ąąF2[Cd], c‚ą£d, andŌĀc (R)(U)=i=0‚ąĎc‚ąí1 0‚Č§j<dj‚Č°i(modc)‚ąĎrjUi.I add a more structural descent form that avoids explicit division by ő¶c, and is especially useful for composite anti-cyclotomic tests such as c=33,57,65.LetAc=F2[Cc]‚ČÉF2 [U]/(Uc‚ąí1),with c odd. For each prime ‚Ąď‚ą£c, define the inflation subspaceInf c/‚Ąďc(Ac/‚Ąď)‚äāAc to be the coefficient functions on Z/cZ that are periodic modulo c/‚Ąď:ri+c/‚Ąď=ri.

Then:the¬†primitive¬†exact-order¬†c¬†component¬†of¬†R¬†vanishes‚üļŌĀc (R)‚ąą‚Ąď‚ą£c‚ąĎInf c/‚Ąďc(Ac/‚Ąď).Proof sketch. OverF2, since c is odd, Ac is semisimple and decomposes by characters U‚Ü¶ő∂j, ő∂ primitive c-th root. The primitive component is the sum over gcd(j,c)=1. A coefficient function periodic modulo c/‚Ąď has Fourier support only on characters with ‚Ąď‚ą£j, becausek=0‚ąĎ‚Ąď‚ąí1(ő∂ jc/‚Ąď)k vanishes unless ő∂ jc/‚Ąď=1, i.e. unless ‚Ąď‚ą£j. The union over primes ‚Ąď‚ą£c is exactly the set of nonprimitive characters. Therefore the sum of these inflation subspaces is precisely the kernel of the primitive c-projection.

This recovers Turn 68‚Äôs prime-power criterion: for c=‚Ąďr, primitive c-vanishing is exactly periodicity modulo ‚Ąď r‚ąí1, i.e.ri=r i+‚Ąďr‚ąí1=‚čĮ=r i+(‚Ąď‚ąí1)‚Ąďr‚ąí1 (0‚Č§i<‚Ąď r‚ąí1).For squarefree c=pq, it gives a much cleaner coefficient test. Identify Z/cZ‚ČÉZ/pZ√óZ/qZ. Then primitive pq-vanishing is equivalent to the existence of functions a on Z/pZ and b on Z/qZ such thatru,v=au+bv.

Equivalently, all 2√ó2 rectangle sums vanish:ru,v+ru‚Ä≤,v+ru,v‚Ä≤+ru‚Ä≤,v‚Ä≤=0.

Thus the next composite tests become explicit:c=33=3‚čÖ11:ru,v=au+bv,u‚ąąZ/3,¬†v‚ąąZ/11.c=65=5‚čÖ13:ru,v=au+bv,u‚ąąZ/5,¬†v‚ąąZ/13.c=57=3‚čÖ19:ru,v=au+bv,u‚ąąZ/3,¬†v‚ąąZ/19.

This is stronger than merely listing ő¶c -divisibility, because it tells the arithmetic comparison exactly what ‚Äúno primitive c-residual‚ÄĚ means at the coefficient level: the residual must descend as a sum of lower-order residuals. If it passes this test, one should not yet declare it zero; one must then descend recursively to the p- and q-components and finally to the invariant component.For the current priority list, this gives a clean recursive residual protocol:c=3,5,11,13,17,19,29,41,43:all coefficients equal, then invariant value zero.c=9,25,27:block-constancy modulo 3,5,9 respectively, then descend.c=33,57,65:rectangle-sum test, then descend to the two prime components.

This is gauge-stable under T‚Ü¶Tu, because unit multiplication permutes characters and carries inflation subspaces to inflation subspaces.So the exact next arithmetic task can be sharpened: compute the normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residual R(T), fold to c, and check whether its primitive part survives by these descent tests. A primitive nonzero branch is a genuine filtered-action obstruction. A primitive-zero but nonzero residual simply means the discrepancy has descended to a lower-order component and must be tested there.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I preserved the stable equationsDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.New this turn: I proved a coefficient-level primitive-descent lemma. For R‚ąąF2[Cd], the primitive exact-order c component of ŌĀc (R) vanishes iff ŌĀc (R) lies in ‚ąĎ‚Ąď‚ą£cInf c/‚ĄďcF2[Cc/‚Ąď]. For squarefree c=pq, this is the explicit rectangle condition ru,v+ru‚Ä≤,v+ru,v‚Ä≤+ru‚Ä≤,v‚Ä≤=0.

GAP: No actual arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has been computed. The presentation is still not proved. The missing step remains either a dyadic formation theorem or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic showing that the true filtered tame action has zero residual in every primitive anti-cyclotomic component.

NEXT: Compute the normalized arithmetic residuals first for c=3,5,9,11. When composite cases enter, use the new descent tests: for c=33,57,65, first test rectangle sums, then descend to the prime components and invariant value.[BREAKTHROUGH]
